Dear A
Thank You for Your Trust

Thank you for your interest. I have crafted this personalised itinerary based on your preferences.

Day by Day
Your Detailed Itinerary
Day 01
Dunhuang Arrival
▾
🗺Arrive at Dunhuang Mogao International Airport (DNH) or Dunhuang Railway Station → Pick‑up → Transfer to hotel → Free time
✈ TransportPrivate car
🏨 Hotellocal 4 star level
🍽 MealsBreakfast
Warm welcome by your local guide and driver.
Comfortable transfer to your hotel (quasi‑4 star).
Evening free to explore Dunhuang’s night markets (e.g., Shazhou Night Market) and taste local specialties like donkey meat noodles, apricot peel tea, and red willow barbecue.

Upon arrival in Dunhuang, your guide will greet you and escort you to your hotel. Dunhuang was a vital oasis on the ancient Silk Road, known for its Buddhist cave art and desert scenery. After check‑in, you can rest or visit the famous Shazhou Night Market on your own (transport and meals at your own expense).

Day 02
Day 2
Dunhuang – Mogao Caves & Mingsha Mountain Crescent Lake
▾
🗺Hotel breakfast → Drive 0.5h to Mogao Caves → Explore the caves (approx. 2h, ticket not included) → Drive 0.5h to Mingsha Mountain & Crescent Lake → Free time for camel riding, sand sliding → Return to hotel
✈ TransportPrivate car
🏨 Hotellocal 4 star level
🍽 MealsBreakfast
Mogao Caves — Known as the “Thousand Buddha Grottoes,” this UNESCO site features exquisite murals and statues. You will first watch two films at the digital center for an introduction, then tour representative caves with a professional guide (ticket must be booked in advance).
Mingsha Mountain & Crescent Lake — A golden sand mountain that “sings” when the wind blows, and a crescent‑shaped spring that has existed for centuries. Optional activities: camel riding (approx. CNY 130/person), sand sliding, or climbing the dunes for a panoramic view.

Morning: After breakfast, drive to the Mogao Caves. Your guide will help you arrange tickets (if not pre‑booked, subject to availability). The caves are the crown jewel of Dunhuang. You will see the famous nine‑story pagoda, the Library Cave (Cave 17), and astonishing murals of flying apsaras (Apsaras). Please note: photography is strictly prohibited inside the caves.

Lunch is on your own (not included).

Afternoon: Visit Mingsha Mountain and Crescent Lake. You can ride a camel up the dunes (recommended) or walk on the wooden path. The view of the spring, shaped like a crescent moon, surrounded by endless sand, is magical. Try sand sliding or simply enjoy the desert sunset (seasonal).

Evening: Return to your hotel. Optionally, you can watch the large‑scale live performance “Encore Dunhuang” or “Dynamic Dunhuang” (tickets from CNY 298/person, not included).

Day 03
Day 3
Dunhuang → Guazhou → Jiayuguan
▾
🗺Hotel breakfast → Drive 1.5h to Guazhou → Visit “Child of the Earth” & “Boundless” sculptures → Continue drive 4.5h to Jiayuguan → Jiayuguan Fort (including shuttle bus) → Check into Jiayuguan hotel
✈ TransportPrivate car
🏨 Hotellocal 4 star level
🍽 MealsBreakfast
Child of the Earth (大地之子) — A 15‑meter‑long red sandstone sculpture of a sleeping baby, lying in the vast Gobi Desert. It raises awareness of environmental protection and the relationship between humans and the Earth.
Boundless (无界) — A white steel structure inspired by Tang Dynasty paradise paintings. It appears like a shimmering mirage floating on the desert, especially beautiful under the setting sun.
Jiayuguan Fort — The “First and Greatest Pass under Heaven.” Built in 1372, this fortress guarded the western end of the Ming Great Wall. Walk on the ramparts, see the Overhanging Great Wall, and enjoy views of the snow‑capped Qilian Mountains.

Morning: Drive to Guazhou. In the Gobi Desert, you will encounter two remarkable modern art installations: “Child of the Earth” – a giant baby sleeping on the ground, evoking a sense of wonder and fragility; and “Boundless” – a bright white structure made of thousands of steel pipes, creating an illusion of a floating city. These have become iconic photo spots along the Hexi Corridor.

Lunch is on your own.

Afternoon: Continue driving to Jiayuguan (approx. 4.5 hours). Visit the Jiayuguan Fort, the best‑preserved pass of the Ming Great Wall. The fortress has an inner and outer city, and the gate tower bears the inscription “First and Greatest Pass under Heaven.” From the wall, you can see the desert and distant mountains. Your guide will explain its strategic importance and the legend of the “splitting brick” that tested the builder’s skill.

Evening: Check into your hotel in Jiayuguan.

Day 04
Day 4
Jiayuguan → Zhangye – Rainbow Danxia & Giant Buddha Temple
▾
🗺Hotel breakfast → Drive 3h to Zhangye → Zhangye Danxia National Geological Park (incl. shuttle bus) → Lunch (own expense) → Giant Buddha Temple (Dafo Temple) → Check into Zhangye hotel
✈ TransportPrivate car
🏨 HotelZhangye local 4 star hotel
🍽 MealsBreakfast
Zhangye Danxia Landform — A UNESCO World Heritage candidate, famous for its rainbow‑colored layered rocks. The park has four main viewing platforms. Platform 1 offers the widest view, Platform 2 the highest panorama, and Platforms 3 & 4 showcase the most vibrant colors, especially under sunlight.
Giant Buddha Temple — Built in the Western Xia Dynasty (1098 AD), it houses the largest reclining indoor Buddha in China (34.5 meters long). The temple also contains rare Buddhist scriptures written in gold and silver ink.

Morning: Drive to Zhangye. Upon arrival, enter the Zhangye Danxia National Geological Park. You will take park shuttle buses to several platforms. The rainbow‑colored hills were formed by the deposition of different minerals over 24 million years – a spectacular sight. Bright clothing (red, yellow) makes for great photos.

Lunch on your own.

Afternoon: Visit the Giant Buddha Temple. The main hall is a magnificent wooden structure. Inside, the reclining Buddha is 34.5 meters long (the largest of its kind in China). It depicts the Buddha entering Nirvana, with ten disciples and various heavenly figures carved around him. Also see the 18 arhats in the side halls and a collection of rare Buddhist sutras.

Evening: Check into your hotel in Zhangye.

Day 05
Day 5
Zhangye → Wuwei – Leitai Han Tomb & White Pagoda Temple
▾
🗺Hotel breakfast → Drive 3.5h to Wuwei → Leitai Han Tomb → Lunch (own expense) → White Pagoda Temple (Baita Temple) → Check into Wuwei hotel
✈ TransportPrivate car
🏨 HotelWuwei local 4 star hotel
🍽 MealsBreakfast
Leitai Han Tomb — An Eastern Han Dynasty brick tomb (circa 2nd century AD) where the iconic bronze “Galloping Horse Treading on a Flying Swallow” (aka the “Bronze Running Horse”) was discovered. The tomb is built under a rammed earth platform. You can descend into the tomb chambers and see replicas of the bronze army.
White Pagoda Temple — A Tibetan Buddhist monastery complex dating to the 13th century. The white stupa was built to commemorate the Tibetan lama Sakyapa Pandita, who played a key role in the Mongol‑Tibetan alliance. The 35‑meter‑tall pagoda and surrounding halls are peaceful and scenic.

Morning: Drive to Wuwei, a historic city on the Silk Road. Visit Leitai Han Tomb. The tomb is known worldwide for unearthing the bronze “Galloping Horse” (now in Gansu Provincial Museum). You will see the original tomb structure, which is a large brick chamber. A replica of the bronze horse and its carriage stands at the site for display.

Lunch on your own.

Afternoon: Visit White Pagoda Temple. The towering Tibetan‑style white pagoda is the centerpiece. The temple complex also includes a museum about Sakya Pandita and the early history of Tibetan Buddhism in the Hexi region. Stroll through the gardens and admire the ancient architecture.

Evening: Check into your hotel in Wuwei.

Day 06
Day 6
Wuwei → Jingtai → Lanzhou – Yellow River Stone Forest & Yellow River Waterfront
▾
🗺Hotel breakfast → Drive 3.5h to Jingtai → Yellow River Stone Forest (incl. entrance & sightseeing cart/boat) → Lunch (own expense) → Drive 2.5h to Lanzhou → Yellow River Waterfront Promenade (Zhongshan Bridge, Yellow River Mother Statue) → Check into Lanzhou hotel
✈ TransportPrivate car
🏨 HotelLanzhou local 4 star hotel
🍽 MealsBreakfast
Yellow River Stone Forest — A 9‑km‑long canyon with towering stone pillars (some over 100 meters high). You will take an electric cart through the valley, then a boat or bamboo raft on the Yellow River to view the cliffs from the water. The scenery is dramatic and less crowded.
Yellow River Waterfront Promenade — Lanzhou‘s iconic riverside boulevard. See the Zhongshan Bridge (first iron bridge over the Yellow River, built in 1909), the Yellow River Mother Statue (symbolizing the river as the mother of Chinese civilization), and the Waterwheel Garden.

Morning: Drive to Jingtai County and enter the Yellow River Stone Forest. The canyon is a natural masterpiece of wind and water erosion. You’ll ride an electric cart into the canyon, then transfer to a small boat or bamboo raft to cruise along the Yellow River, surrounded by towering stone columns. Keep your camera ready – the reflections in the water are stunning.

Lunch on your own.

Afternoon: Continue driving to Lanzhou. Upon arrival, walk along the Yellow River Waterfront Promenade. The Zhongshan Bridge is a great spot for photos. The Yellow River Mother Statue shows a mother and child, representing the nurturing role of the river. Enjoy the local atmosphere; you might see people practicing tai chi or flying kites along the riverbank.

Evening: Check into your hotel in Lanzhou.

Day 07
Day 7
Lanzhou – Gansu Provincial Museum & High‑Speed Train to Dunhuang (Desert Star Room)
▾
🗺Hotel breakfast → Gansu Provincial Museum → Lunch (own expense) → Transfer to Lanzhou West Station → High‑speed train to Dunhuang (reference trains: D2749 11:58-19:26 or D2737 13:47-21:00, approx. 7.5 hours) → Arrive Dunhuang → Transfer to Desert Star Room Hotel (Xingkong Hotel) → Overnight
✈ TransportHigh-speed train
🏨 HotelDesert Star Room Hotel (Xingkong Hotel)
🍽 MealsBreakfast
Gansu Provincial Museum — A national first‑class museum. Must‑see exhibits: the Bronze Galloping Horse (the tourism symbol of China), ancient silk textiles, a replica of a Mogao Cave, and a dinosaur fossil hall.
High‑speed train journey — Travel across the Hexi Corridor, passing by the Qilian Mountains, vast grasslands, and desert landscapes. A unique experience of the “Silk Road Express.”
Desert Star Room (Xingkong Hotel) — Located at the foot of Mingsha Mountain, you’ll stay in a special room with a glass dome ceiling (? - actually it’s a luxury desert hotel with star‑viewing facilities). Enjoy the starry sky over the dunes. (Note: The package includes this special accommodation.)

Morning: Visit the Gansu Provincial Museum. The museum is a treasure house of Silk Road history. The highlight is the Bronze Galloping Horse (Eastern Han Dynasty), famously depicted leaping with one hoof resting on a flying swallow. Other halls showcase beautiful murals, Buddhist statues, and the “Yellow River Ancient Elephant” fossil.

Lunch on your own.

Afternoon: Transfer to Lanzhou West Station and board a high‑speed train to Dunhuang. The journey is long (about 7.5 hours) but comfortable. You will see how the landscape changes from green to desert. Snacks and drinks are recommended.

Evening: Arrive at Dunhuang. Your guide will transfer you to the Desert Star Room Hotel (Xingkong Hotel). This hotel offers a unique experience: you are close to the Mingsha Mountain dunes, and on clear nights you can see

Day 08
Day 8
Dunhuang – Yadan Devil City, Yumen Pass & Silk Road Heritage City – Departure
▾
🗺Hotel breakfast → Drive 1.5h to Yadan National Geopark (Yadan Devil City) → Explore the yardang formations (incl. shuttle bus) → Drive 1h to Yumen Pass (Jade Gate Pass) → Drive 1h to Silk Road Heritage City (incl. shuttle bus) → Transfer to Dunhuang airport/railway station
✈ TransportPrivate car
🏨 Hotelnon
🍽 MealsBreakfast
Yadan Devil City — A spectacular wind-eroded desert landscape. The rock formations resemble castles, pagodas, ships, and animals. When wind blows, it makes eerie howling sounds — hence “Devil City.” The shuttle bus takes you to several viewpoints.
Yumen Pass — Also called “Jade Gate Pass.” A Han Dynasty frontier post where jade from Central Asia entered China. A lone rammed-earth watchtower (the Small Square City) stands in the vast desert — a poignant symbol of the Silk Road.
Silk Road Heritage City — A 6,000-acre film studio and cultural park recreating ancient Silk Road scenes: replicas of the Forbidden City, Tang dynasty palaces, Hakka earthen buildings, Egyptian temples, and more. Enjoy a “walk through time” experience.

Morning: After breakfast (at your own expense), drive to Yadan National Geopark (Yadan Devil City). This area is a classic example of yardang landforms formed by wind erosion. You will take a shuttle bus through the park, stopping at spots like “Pirate Ship,” “Golden Lion Welcoming Guests,” and “Beacon Tower.” The landscape is desolate, beautiful, and photogenic. Bring water, sunblock, and a scarf to protect against dust.

Lunch on your own (pack some snacks).

Afternoon: Visit Yumen Pass – just a single tower ruin, but full of historical resonance. Your guide will tell stories of ancient travelers and soldiers. Then drive to Silk Road Heritage City, where you can walk through replicas of famous world landmarks and ancient Chinese cities. It’s a fun spot for photography and learning about Silk Road history.

Late afternoon: Transfer to Dunhuang airport or railway station for your departure flight/train. Your 8-day Hexi Corridor tour ends here.

What's Covered
Inclusions & Exclusions
✅ Included
  • ✓  Quasi-4-star hotel accommodation: Dunhuang 2 nights, Jiayuguan 1 night, Zhangye 1 night, Wuwei 1 night, Lanzhou 1 night, Dunhuang (Desert Star Room / Xingkong Hotel) 1 night
  • ✓  English-speaking local guide and private driver throughout the 7-day tour
  • ✓  All airport/railway station transfers by private car in Dunhuang
  • ✓  All intercity road transfers by private car: Dunhuang → Guazhou → Jiayuguan, Jiayuguan → Zhangye, Zhangye → Wuwei, Wuwei → Jingtai → Lanzhou
  • ✓  High-speed train ticket Lanzhou West → Dunhuang (2nd class, approx. 7.5 hours)
  • ✓  All entrance fees and internal transport as specified: Mogao Caves (guide-assisted visit; ticket booking support provided), Mingsha Mountain & Crescent Lake (entrance), Jiayuguan Fort (incl. shuttle bus), Zhangye Danxia National Geological Park (incl. shuttle bus), Leitai Han Tomb, White Pagoda Temple, Yellow River Stone Forest (incl. entrance, sightseeing cart, and boat/raft), Gansu Provincial Museum
  • ✓  Daily breakfast at hotels
  • ✓  Travel insurance covering medical expenses and emergency evacuation (standard coverage per Chinese outbound tour operator requirements)
❌ Not Included
  • ✗  International or domestic flights to Dunhuang
  • ✗  Chinese entry visa and related application fees
  • ✗  Mogao Caves entrance tickets (must be pre-booked separately; not included due to strict quota control)
  • ✗  All lunches and dinners (except daily breakfast)
  • ✗  Optional activities: camel riding at Mingsha Mountain (CNY 130/person), sand sliding, climbing dunes, live performances 'Encore Dunhuang' or 'Dynamic Dunhuang' (from CNY 298/person)
  • ✗  Personal expenses (shopping, laundry, beverages, tips, etc.)
  • ✗  Gratuities for guide and driver
  • ✗  Photography fees or special permits (e.g., professional camera equipment at heritage sites)
  • ✗  Travel insurance beyond standard coverage (e.g., trip cancellation, baggage loss, higher medical limits)
